---
title: "Creating A Sub Category"
---

---
title: "Creating a Sub-Category"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Governance/sjo1747998323583"
section: "Hyland Governance"
---

A sub-category serves the same function as a category The structure in the file plan simplifies category management. You can

To create a sub-category:

1.  Open your project in Studio Modeler.
1.  Go to All Governance projects under  Governance Configuration.
2.  Select your project. The Governance project site is displayed.
2.  Click the vertical ellipsis next to the category or sub-category for which you want to create a sub-category.
3.  Select Create Sub Category. The Create Category window is displayed. !['Create category' window showing the parent category.](data:image/png;base64,)
4.  Enter a category name in the Category Name field.  Note: A parent category and a sub-category can have the same name. The non-editable Parent Category text field shows the location of the new sub-category.
5.  Click OK. The sub-category is created under the category in File Plan Categories. A side panel containing Overview and Retention sections is displayed on the right side of the screen.
6.  Select a Record Type to set a content type that will be used for the category. The Record Type section contains a dropdown listing Governable Content Types in the selected Governance Project.   Note: For more information on content types, see Content Types in the Hyland Automate Modeling documentation and Terminology.   A new sub-category is created and the Governance project site is displayed. The sub-category is added to the File Plan Categories.
7.  Set the retention policy for the sub-category. To create a retention policy, see Creating a Retention Policy.
If the sub-category does not have a retention policy, it is labeled in the categories list with a status Not configured.

8.  Click Save. The new sub-category is created and displayed on the categories' list. Under the Category Name  you can see the path pointing where the sub-category is placed.
